sourceMovie.title: Annabelle
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Annabelle works as a horror movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Halloween
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Annabelle clicked for you, Halloween (2018) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Annabelle?
faq.answer: Try Halloween, Insidious: Chapter 2, Insidious: Chapter 3—each shares DNA with Annabelle while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Annabelle?
faq.answer: Annabelle (2014) is best described as Horror. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Annabelle part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Annabelle Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
